data.title: Communications Services Down In Quiet Trading Session — Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
The Communication Services sector, which includes Mag 7 names such as Alphabet, Meta and Amazon.com, declined in thin trade squeeze between Christmas and the weekend.

Netflix edged higher, while Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery both lost ground as investors wait for updates in the ongoing merger battle. 




  Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com 



  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  December 26, 2025 18:00 ET (23:00 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Utilities Shares Slip Ahead Of Winter Storms — Utilities Roundup
data.description:
Utility stocks were among the top losers Friday, as the northeast states braced for a winter storm.

U.S. bond markets traded sideways, with yields slightly lower, following the Christmas holiday. Investors are mostly betting on an interest rate hold by the Federal Reserve in January. 




  Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com 



  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  December 26, 2025 18:03 ET (23:03 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Energy Shares Follow Oil Prices Lower — Energy Roundup
data.description:
Energy shares were among the top losers Friday as oil futures declined, weighed down by renewed hopes of a Ukraine/Russia peace deal as Ukrainian leader Zelensky heads out for a meeting with President Trump in Mar-A-Lago over the weekend.

Sable Offshore stock tumbled after environmental groups filed a lawsuit in federal court on Wednesday to block the restart of oil pipelines near Santa Barbara, Calif. 




  Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com 



  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  December 26, 2025 17:39 ET (22:39 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Materials Shares Rise On Silver, Gold — Materials Roundup
data.description:
Materials stocks were among the few gainers among S&P 500 sectors, as silver and gold futures extended their rally amid escalating geopolitical tensions.

Silver futures have climbed 164% this year, blowing through a 45-year record in October. That included a jump of 7.7% on Friday alone, to notch the largest one-day and one-week dollar increases on record.

Freeport-McMoRan was named in a class-action lawsuit alleging the company misled investors by concealing inadequate safety at its Grasberg Block Cave mine in Indonesia. 




  Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com 



  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  December 26, 2025 17:42 ET (22:42 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 PM ET: Silver and Gold Prices Rally; Stocks Hold Near Records | Zelensky ...
data.description:

Indexes back away slightly from records hit Wednesday.

Zelensky to Meet Trump on Closing Gaps in Draft Peace Deal

The high level talks come after a flurry of U.S.-Ukraine negotiations, but Russia remains a possible obstacle.

----

----

China Sanctions Boeing, Other U.S. Companies Over Taiwan Arms Sale

Beijing responded to the Trump administration's approval of a large weapons package for Taipei with restrictions on firms and executives.

----

Silver's Runaway Rally Sweeps Up Amateur Investors

----

Japan Plans Record High Initial Budget for Next Fiscal Year

The Japanese cabinet approved a record-high initial budget totaling approximately $785 billion for the coming fiscal year, signaling Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's latest effort to support growth through aggressive spending.

----

The Economic Divide Between Big and Small Companies Is Growing

The economic fortunes of low- and high-income Americans are diverging. The same pattern is happening with companies.

----

Japan Closer to Achieving 2% Inflation Target, BOJ Governor Says

----

----

data.title: NetEase Interactive Entertainment Group Head to Retire
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall 




  NetEase's head of its interactive entertainment group, Yingfeng Ding, is retiring at the end of the year. 



  Ding, who also holds an executive vice president title, will retire on Dec. 31 after 23 years at the Chinese internet and video games company. 



  He will remain a consultant to the company in 2026, NetEase said Friday. 



  The company did not share information about a search for Ding's successor. 





  Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  December 26, 2025 16:37 ET (21:37 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.
